import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut cur = ""
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    let bopt = string.byte_at(s, i)
    match bopt
    when Some(b)
      if b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 13 or b == 10
        if not string.is_empty(cur)
          match string.parse_int(cur)
          when Some(v)
            result = list.push(result, v)
          when None
            ()
          end
          cur = ""
        end
      else
        cur = "#{cur}#{string.from_byte(b)}"
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if not string.is_empty(cur)
    match string.parse_int(cur)
    when Some(v)
      result = list.push(result, v)
    when None
      ()
    end
  end
  result
end

fn get_or_zero(_ xs: List<Int>, _ i: Int) -> Int
  match xs.get(i)
  when Some(v)
    v
  when None
    0
  end
end

fn main() -> Unit
  let line1 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
  let line2 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
  let nums = parse_ints(line1)
  let target = match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
  when Some(v)
    v
  when None
    0
  end
  let n = nums.len()
  mut found = false
  mut fi = 0
  mut fj = 0
  mut i = 0
  while i < n and not found
    mut j = i + 1
    while j < n and not found
      let ai = get_or_zero(nums, i)
      let aj = get_or_zero(nums, j)
      if ai + aj == target
        found = true
        fi = i
        fj = j
      end
      j = j + 1
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if found
    println("found=#{fi},#{fj}")
  else
    println("none")
  end
end
